mechanism_narrative: High personal consumption expenditure levels, as measured by RSAFS, directly reflect strong aggregate demand. Sustained high demand drives corporate revenues and earnings, which supports elevated S&P 500 valuations and positive forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of RSAFS level on 20-day forward returns is below 0.02, or the mechanism fails in periods of recession-defined negative GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 0.25%, it indicates an extreme easing stance by the Federal Reserve, often associated with quantitative easing and forward guidance. Such aggressive accommodation compresses risk premiums and lifts equity prices as investors anticipate prolonged low rates. This threshold condition triggers a positive equity return signal over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative in more than 60% of instances, or the average return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of extremely high unemployment (UNRATE > 7%) and near-zero interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) defines a post-crisis environment where monetary policy is maximally accommodative and labor market slack is ample. In such conditions, equity markets anticipate a strong recovery in employment and consumer spending, driving positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon as the output gap closes.
failure_condition: When both threshold conditions are met, the 60-day forward return is negative or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) over any 10-year period containing at least 10 such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real output. In high-unemployment regimes (UNRATE > 8%), rising INDPRO signals a recovery in production capacity and aggregate supply, which is interpreted positively by equity markets as it forecasts improving economic conditions. In low-unemployment regimes (UNRATE < 4%), rising INDPRO may indicate overheating and trigger inflation expectations, leading to negative forward returns. Currently, with UNRATE at 10.2%, we are in a high-unemployment regime, so the mechanism predicts positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO changes and 20-day forward returns is positive in low-unemployment regimes or negative in high-unemployment regimes in any 5-year window, the hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: The level of nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) directly indicates the number of people employed, representing labor market strength. Higher employment supports consumer income and spending, thereby increasing corporate earnings expectations. This positive income effect translates into higher equity prices over the short term, regardless of the broader economic regime.
failure_condition: The weekly (5-day) forward return following high PAYEMS readings (top quartile historically) is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) compared to low PAYEMS readings over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,150 thousand units indicate robust residential investment, which has significant multiplier effects on employment, construction materials demand, and consumer durables. When starts cross this threshold, it signals strong underlying demand and positive economic momentum, leading to superior S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1150, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is negative more than 50% of the time or the mean return is not positive over a 20-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong real personal consumption (RSAFS > 500,000) and near-zero interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates an ideal environment for equity markets. High demand indicates consumer strength, while low rates reduce the cost of capital and discount rates, amplifying profit margins and valuations. This interaction effect is stronger than the sum of individual effects and predicts positive forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: In periods meeting both conditions, the interaction term in a regression of 20-day forward returns on RSAFS and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) or the combined signal has lower Sharpe ratio than individual signals over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic activity. In a post-recession environment with high unemployment (UNRATE > 8%), a higher level of GDP indicates a larger economy with more growth potential, leading to positive forward returns. In a mature expansion with low unemployment (UNRATE < 4%), a higher GDP level may be associated with increased risk of inflation and monetary tightening, leading to negative forward returns. Given current UNRATE of 10.2%, we are in the high-unemployment regime, so the mechanism predicts positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the regression coefficient of GDPC1 level on 60-day forward returns is positive when UNRATE < 4% or negative when UNRATE > 8% over a 20-year sample, the hypothesis is falsified.
